message:
fix(websocket): eliminate write-buffer copies and make pong draining cancel-safe

The client, server, and split WebSocket writers all shared the same
anti-pattern: encode a frame into write_buf, copy write_buf.to_vec(),
then pass the Vec to a standalone write_all_io helper. This doubled
memory traffic on every outgoing frame.

Replace the copy-then-write pattern with an in-place loop that calls
poll_write directly on write_buf.chunk() and advances via Buf::advance.
This removes the write_all_io/write_all helpers entirely.

Additionally, pending pong draining switched from
std::mem::take(&mut self.pending_pongs) + for loop to
while let Some(payload) = self.pending_pongs.pop(). The old mem::take
approach was not cancel-safe: if the future was dropped between the
take and the completion of the last pong send, the remaining pongs
were silently lost. The pop-based loop ensures each pong is removed
from the queue only as it is consumed.

In split.rs, send_frame_internal and WriteHalf send_frame now encode
into the shared write_buf and delegate to flush_write_buf instead of
materializing a temporary Vec.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
